mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and builder confidence, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive change in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth below 1.5 percent reflects insufficient aggregate demand to sustain corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with a declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting manufacturing recovery, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising INDPRO and falling FEDFUNDS yields a negative information coefficient over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and rising household income, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant predictive power for 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 230, accelerating inflation expectations force monetary authorities to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL surpasses 230.
